what_input_feature: Input must reach png_XYZ_from_xy with a green chromaticity field whose fixed-point value satisfies greenx<0 OR greenx>PNG_FP_1 (100000 = 0x000186A0), i.e. the multi-byte greenx operand must be pushed above the 100000 magnitude threshold so the line-1288 guard takes its true side and returns 1.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile_cmplog winner reaches line 1288 and takes the true side (W shows T=1 F=0 at L1288 while the loser shows T=0 F=40), resolves the branch in 9/10 trials, and accumulates 56 hits versus the loser's 3 — its chromaticity bytes land greenx outside the [0,PNG_FP_1] window.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser only ever takes the false side at L1288 (T=0 F=40), blocks in 8/10 trials, and never drives greenx above the 100000 threshold; it stalls 21.10h on the branch with only 3 hits, so its green chromaticity stays inside the in-range window.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers carry the same input-to-state substitution mutator (cmplog and vpc are identical except for value_profile), so the comparison-solving mutator is held constant; the winning technique is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient. The comparison greenx>PNG_FP_1 is a magnitude inequality against the multi-byte constant 100000 (0x000186A0) that literal-substitution mutation cannot directly solve, but the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward inputs whose greenx operand creeps progressively closer to and past 100000, giving the gradient that lets vpc overshoot the threshold where plain cmplog cannot.

what_input_feature: A DOCTYPE internal-subset body whose unescaped scan reaches a ']' character (setting the scanner state machine to state==']') and then a following '>' / blank, i.e. a `]` closing sequence inside the parsed DTD section. Reaching the BLOCKER true side at parser.c:11261 requires the byte stream to advance the state machine through '<' / quote / ']' transitions until state holds ']'.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile_cmplog winner resolves 9/10 and actually enters state==']' (W shows T=4 at L11261 and downstream W-only execution of the `if (*cur == '>')`, `IS_BLANK_CH`, and state==' ' arms at 11262-11286). Its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that incrementally match the multi-byte DTD structure, so the scanner state machine is steered into the ']' state.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser blocks 8/10 and never sets state==']' — L shows T=0 at L11261 and never executes the 11262-11286 W-only arms; its scan stalls in the generic state-tracking arms (state!=0, '<', quote) without reaching the ']' close. Lacking CMP_MAP gradient, it gets no partial-progress credit for assembling the ']'-bearing internal subset.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets: each intercepted comparison contributes Hamming/prefix-distance feedback so inputs that partially match the multi-byte DTD ']'-close structure are retained and refined. cmplog (I2S only, no CMP_MAP) gets no such gradient credit, so it cannot incrementally walk the scanner state machine into state==']'. The delta between the two involved fuzzers is exactly value_profile, so the winning mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient.

what_input_feature: A well-formed XML prologue followed by a DOCTYPE whose internal-subset region presents an unescaped quote or ']' character to the chunk-scanner at line 11304 while the state machine is in the neutral state (state==0), so the scanner enters the quoted/bracket state instead of bailing at an earlier dispatch.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds drive the scanner into the bracket/space sub-states the loser never reaches: at line 11261 the winner takes the ']' state True 4 times and at line 11275 the ' ' state True 12 times, and at line 11304 the winner records True hits (T=4) whereas the loser stays at T=0; the winning seed carries an intact '<?xml version="1.0"?>' / '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "' structure that feeds the DTD scanner.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser (cmplog) seeds never enter the ']' or ' ' end-detection states (L11261 T=0, L11275 T=0) and never take the blocker's True side (L11304 T=0 F=84); the loser seed's DOCTYPE is corrupted ('a S[STEM') so the scanner does not arrive at the internal-subset quote/bracket dispatch in the neutral state.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient buckets reward inputs whose comparison operands are progressively closer (Hamming/prefix distance) to the structural literals the DTD scanner expects, smoothing the path to a quote/']' arriving while state==0; cmplog alone (input-to-state substitution plus only edge feedback, no CMP_MAP gradient) gets a flat edge signal and cannot retain the partial-match progress, so it stalls at the entry of the internal-subset scan. The winner-vs-loser delta is precisely the value_profile gradient feedback.

what_input_feature: Input must reach an <!ELEMENT> declaration whose content model is a multi-alternative/sequence group (e.g. mixed content '(a|b|...)' or a SEQ/OR tree) so that during the bottom-up free walk a freed child equals parent->c2 rather than parent->c1, exercising the false side at valid.c:1125.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the false branch in 8/10 trials and actually reaches line 1127/1128 (parent->c2 = NULL) marked [W], i.e. it constructs element-content trees with a right child c2; its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards partial matches of the multi-byte DTD keyword/punctuation prefixes ('<!ELEMENT', '(', '|') letting it incrementally build the deeper content-model grammar that produces a c2 sibling.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks in 9/10 trials with hitcount 0 on the branch; it reaches line 1125 only on the true side (c1) and never produces a content model with a second child, so parent->c2 stays unset and the false branch is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance feedback) gives partial credit for byte sequences approaching the DTD content-model tokens, so value_profile_cmplog assembles the multi-child '(a|b|...)' group that cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot, yielding a c2 child and the false branch.

what_input_feature: Byte content that progressively matches the multi-clause comparison cascade routing into the validation path (DOCTYPE/SYSTEM keyword bytes and the attribute-type switch), so the input is rewarded for partial matches toward reaching xmlIsDocNameChar's true branch.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets so partial-match progress on the string/integer comparisons along the chain (switch type at xmlValidateAttributeValueInternal L3865, name-char clauses) is retained as new feedback, letting the fuzzer climb toward the true side and resolve in 8/10 trials (winner: resolved=8/10).
why_loser_doesnt: Plain cmplog has no CMP_MAP gradient signal — only edge buckets plus I2S substitution — so it gets no credit for partial progress on the comparison cascade and stalls blocked in 9/10 trials (loser: resolved=1/10 blocked=9) with far fewer true-branch hits (avg hitcount on branch: winner=99 loser=8).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance feedback buckets) rewards inputs for partial comparison matches along the validation chain, giving the fuzzer a smooth climb toward the name-char true branch that plain cmplog's binary edge feedback does not provide.

what_input_feature: Three consecutive buffer codepoints encoding 0x0930 (RA) at the current glyph, then 0x094D (virama) at cur(1), then 0x0907 at cur(2), with idx+2 < count so the third glyph exists. In the harness input these appear as little-endian 32-bit glyph words 30 09 00 00 / 4d 09 00 00 followed by the third codepoint slot.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed begins 30 09 00 00 4d 09 00 00 06 00 00 00 — the first glyph word is 0x0930 (entering case 0x0930) and the second word is 0x094D (the virama required by cur(1)), supplying the leading two operands of the three-way conjunction that the loser never satisfies; the winner takes the true side (L94 W T=1 F=14 vs L T=0 F=149).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry unrelated bytes at those slots (e.g. 19 09 00 00, 00 00 00 00, sn-hant/zh- ASCII tag strings) so they never co-locate 0x0930, 0x094D and 0x0907 at adjacent glyph positions; across 10 loser trials case 0x0930 stays false (L94 L T=0 F=149) and the branch is blocked for 18.80h.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog's I2S. The blocker is a conjunction of three exact 32-bit codepoint equalities; CMP_MAP's Hamming/prefix-distance feedback rewards inputs that match MORE of the three operands partially (e.g. getting cur()==0x0930 and cur(1)==0x094D before cur(2)==0x0907), giving an intermediate-credit gradient that cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot see, so vpc climbs the multi-equality gate that cmplog stalls on.

what_input_feature: A glyph stream whose current codepoint equals the exact 16-bit Gurmukhi constant 0x0A72, entered only after the outer switch has already selected the HB_SCRIPT_GURMUKHI arm; the loser must produce this same 0x0A72 codepoint that gates the case label at line 144.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in all 10 trials (avg 3.70h) and the per-role overlay marks the Gurmukhi arm (lines 130-163) plus the blocker case 0x0A72u as [B]/hit on the winner side, with the inner switch at line 145 taken True 7 times for W versus only 1 for L.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks the branch in 8/10 trials (avg 20.10h, hitcount 0); having only I2S without the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient it cannot incrementally close in on the exact 0x0A72 codepoint, so it almost never satisfies the case label.
mechanism_attribution: Both involved fuzzers carry the same input-to-state comparison-solving mutator, so that technique is held constant; the only technique delta is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient buckets, which reward Hamming/prefix progress toward the 0x0A72 codepoint comparison and let the winner climb to the exact match the loser cannot reach by comparison-solving alone.

what_input_feature: A glyph buffer whose script resolves to HB_SCRIPT_GURMUKHI and that contains an adjacent codepoint pair where buffer->cur()==0x0A72 and buffer->cur(1) is one of {0x0A3F,0x0A40,0x0A47}, so that `matched` becomes true at the blocker line 162.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog (winner) uniquely reaches `case 0x0A72u:` (line 144 W T=1 vs L T=0) and takes the true side of the blocker (line 162 W T=1 vs L T=0), resolving in all 10 trials, because its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that progressively match the two-codepoint constraint comparison.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (loser) blocks in all 10 trials and never enters the 0x0A72 arm (line 144 L T=0); it iterates the Gurmukhi loop heavily (line 131 L T=300) but only its edge-bucket feedback fires, giving no reward for partial progress toward the exact codepoint pair, so it never assembles the matched pair.
mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between the two involved fuzzers is value_profile: both carry the same input-to-state CMP substitution mutator, but only value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets. The Hamming/prefix-distance value-profile feedback makes near-miss codepoint comparisons (e.g. one byte off from 0x0A72 / 0x0A3F) register as new coverage, steering the corpus toward the exact matched vowel pair that cmplog's edge-only feedback cannot distinguish from any other non-matching glyph.

what_input_feature: A SQL statement whose column reference is a three-part schema-qualified name (X.Y.Z, e.g. db.table.column) so that lookupName is called with a non-NULL zDb; the name must fail to resolve so cnt!=1 and control reaches the error block, where 'if( zDb )' (98156) is then true.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seed carries repeated dotted multi-part qualifier structure (e.g. '.112/1111', 'E.E.E' runs), and the d=2 dispatch shows the winner taking case TK_DOT (L98355 T=1) which builds zDb/zTable/zColumn before calling lookupName; at d=1 the winner takes 'if( zDb )' true (L97837 T=1) and the blocker 'if( zDb )' true (L98156 T=1).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are flat single-token or repeated-letter payloads ('SELECT bbLLLL...', 'SELECT bbbbb') with no nested X.Y dotted qualifier; the loser never builds zDb (d=1 L97837 T=0 F=20, blocker L98156 T=0 F=20) — it routes through the unqualified column path (case TK_ID, L98354 T=20).
mechanism_attribution: The pair delta is value_profile: both fuzzers share the same input-to-state comparison-substitution mutator, but only value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets). Reaching the db-qualified branch requires the input to first survive several near-miss string comparisons (sqlite3StrICmp on db names, schema-name matching) and to emit a syntactically valid dotted reference; the CMP_MAP gradient rewards incremental Hamming progress toward those multi-byte comparison targets, letting vpc assemble the X.Y.Z structure that plain cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot incrementally climb toward.
